description: Step 1 calls `gws docs +write --title ... --body ...`; gws-docs-write requires --document <ID> and --text <TEXT>. Both required flags are absent and --title/--body are not valid flags; command fails at runtime.
suggested_fix: Create doc first with `gws docs documents create`, then call `gws docs +write --document DOC_ID --text '...'` to append content.

description: Step 1 calls `gws forms forms list` but the Google Forms API v1 has no list method on the forms resource (only create, get, batchUpdate, setPublishSettings). Command fails at runtime.
suggested_fix: Remove step 1 or replace with a Drive files list query: `gws drive files list --params '{"q": "mimeType = \"application/vnd.google-apps.form\""}' --format table`.

description: `cat "$1"` reads any file path supplied as the first argument with no validation or restriction, enabling path traversal if invoked from automation with attacker-controlled input.
fp_reason: This is a manually-invoked developer utility for displaying ASCII art; it has no automated call surface and is not reachable by external input in normal usage.
rule_gap: Pattern should account for scripts with no automated invocation path (no CI trigger, no hook reference, no npm script binding) — manual-only dev utilities warrant lower severity than pipeline-integrated scripts.
suggested_fix: Add a guard: validate $1 is within the repo art/ directory before calling cat.

description: `cargo install cargo-llvm-cov` downloads and compiles a crate from crates.io at runtime without a hash pin, allowing supply-chain substitution if the crate name is hijacked or yanked.
suggested_fix: Pre-install cargo-llvm-cov in the CI Docker image or pin with `cargo install --locked cargo-llvm-cov@0.6.x` and verify with a known-good hash.

description: devDependencies use ^ semver: `@changesets/cli ^2.29.8` and `lefthook ^2.1.2` — minor/patch updates apply automatically on fresh installs, bypassing review.
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions (remove ^) and rely on pnpm-lock.yaml for reproducibility; enforce with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI.

description: Description contains vague quantifier: "add members with appropriate roles" — does not specify which roles (writer, reader, commenter) are appropriate for which members.
suggested_fix: Replace "appropriate roles" with a concrete example: "add members as writers or readers".

description: No API Resources section; every other gws-* API reference skill includes this section. Without it, agents cannot discover raw modelarmor resource methods (templates, findings) via the pattern shown in Discovering Commands.
suggested_fix: Add an ## API Resources section listing the modelarmor v1 resources (projects.locations.templates, projects.locations.userManagedModelArmors) and their methods.

description: Step 3 is "Compare the data and identify changes" with no CLI command; leaves an agent with data but no concrete next action to produce a diff output.
suggested_fix: Add a concrete action: pipe both reads through jq or write the comparison result with `gws docs +write`; at minimum note the expected output format.

description: Step 2 is "Copy the text from the body content" — a manual human action with no CLI command. An agent following this recipe has no automated path to extract body text.
suggested_fix: Replace with `gws docs documents get --params '{"documentId": "DOC_ID"}' | jq -r '.body.content[].paragraph.elements[]?.textRun.content' | tr -d '\n'` or equivalent extraction pipeline.

description: Step 2 says "for each row, create a calendar event" but shows only a single hardcoded insert command with static attendees; loop iteration mechanism is not demonstrated.
suggested_fix: Note explicitly that the agent should iterate over each data row from step 1 and substitute real values from the sheet into the calendar insert command per row.

description: Step 3 is "Review due dates and prioritize overdue items" with no CLI command; does not specify how to filter by due date or what action to take on overdue tasks.
suggested_fix: Add filtering guidance: `gws tasks tasks list ... | jq '[.items[] | select(.due < now | todate)]'` or direct the agent to update task status.

description: Step 2 is "Review the output and identify files to archive or move" with no CLI command; provides no path from identification to action.
suggested_fix: Add optional follow-on commands: move to archive folder with `gws drive files update --params '{"fileId": "FILE_ID", "addParents": "ARCHIVE_FOLDER_ID"}'` or delete with `gws drive files delete`.

description: Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
suggested_fix: Verify with `gws docs +write --help` and align to the actual flag name across all recipe files and the skill documentation.

description: Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
suggested_fix: Verify with `gws docs +write --help` and align to the actual flag name across all recipe files and the skill documentation.

description: Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
suggested_fix: Verify with `gws docs +write --help` and align to the actual flag name across all recipe files and the skill documentation.

description: gws-docs-write documents `--document <ID>` as the required flag, but three recipes (recipe-save-email-to-doc, recipe-generate-report-from-sheet, recipe-create-doc-from-template) consistently use `--document-id`. The consistent use across three independently-authored recipes suggests the actual binary flag may be `--document-id` and the skill file is the error source.
suggested_fix: Run `gws docs +write --help` to determine the authoritative flag name, then update gws-docs-write/SKILL.md or all three recipe files to match.

description: new Function(...Object.keys(context), `return ${condition}`) executes a dynamic condition string from template config; functionally equivalent to eval() and vulnerable to code injection if condition values are attacker-influenced
suggested_fix: Replace new Function() with a safe condition evaluator using an explicit allowlist of supported operators (e.g., simple comparison parser) rather than arbitrary JS execution

description: curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash appears in a Claude command file; when /taskmaster:install-taskmaster is invoked Claude may execute this command, fetching and running arbitrary shell code from the network
suggested_fix: Replace curl|bash with explicit instructions to use a package manager (brew install nvm, nvm installer via documented download+verify) or remove the nvm troubleshooting step from the automated command flow

description: sudo npm install -g task-master-ai in a Claude command file; Claude may execute this privileged command on behalf of users without additional confirmation
suggested_fix: Document permission-fix alternatives (npm config set prefix, nvm usage) instead of sudo; do not include sudo commands in executable Claude command files

description: npx -y task-master-ai with no version pin; the -y flag auto-confirms any version from npm registry, so a malicious publish or compromised npm account could run arbitrary code without user review
suggested_fix: Pin to specific version: npx -y task-master-ai@0.43.1 and update intentionally with each release

pattern: semver caret ranges on security-sensitive packages
description: jsonwebtoken ^9.0.2, undici ^7.16.0, express ^4.21.2 and most other production dependencies use ^ ranges; a patch or minor release with a vulnerability will be auto-installed on npm install
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions for jsonwebtoken, undici, express; add npm audit to CI pipeline

pattern: missing version field in plugin manifest
description: plugin.json has no version field; standard Claude Code plugin manifests require a semantic version for install and update operations
suggested_fix: Add "version": "0.43.1" (or current package version) to plugin.json

description: Frontmatter block has allowed-tools and description but no name field; command registration relies on filename-derived name
suggested_fix: Add name: dedupe to the YAML frontmatter block

pattern: no frontmatter; required $ARGUMENTS with no empty-input guard
description: No YAML frontmatter; command requires a PR number via $ARGUMENTS but has no guard for empty invocation, leading to silent or confusing failure
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter with name, description, allowed-tools; add explicit empty-argument check at step 1

description: No YAML frontmatter at all; command runs with default broad tool permissions
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter with name: ham, description, and allowed-tools scoped to Bash(tm *) and relevant tools

description: No YAML frontmatter; command file lacks name, description, and allowed-tools declarations
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter with name: goham, description, allowed-tools scoped to Bash(tm *), Bash(git *), Bash(gh *)

pattern: systemic missing name frontmatter across all 47 plugin commands
description: All 47 command files in packages/claude-code-plugin/commands/ lack a YAML frontmatter name field; plugin registration depends entirely on filename-derived naming
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter block with name, description, and allowed-tools to each command file; can be done as a single bulk PR

description: tm-main.md documents /taskmaster:generate -> generate-tasks but no generate-tasks.md or generate.md command file exists in the plugin commands directory
suggested_fix: Either create generate-tasks.md command or remove the /taskmaster:generate entry from tm-main.md

description: install-taskmaster.md says 'Run /project:utils:check-health to verify setup' but no such command file exists in the plugin
suggested_fix: Remove the reference or create the check-health command

description: learn.md uses /project:task-master:* namespace (e.g., /project:task-master:init, /project:task-master:next) but the installed plugin namespace is /taskmaster:*; all 6+ cross-references use incorrect prefix
suggested_fix: Replace all /project:task-master: prefixes with /taskmaster: to match actual installed command paths

description: Command has no specified output format; states it will chain commands contextually but provides no contract for what Claude must produce
suggested_fix: Add an Output Format section specifying what the command should return (e.g., selected workflow name, commands executed, summary of results)

description: Pipeline command defines input syntax extensively but never specifies what output format to produce after execution
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section specifying execution summary, step results, and final state report

description: No specified output format contract; shows UI mockup examples but these are illustrative, not prescribed
suggested_fix: Specify whether output must match the mockup format or is purely illustrative; add explicit Output Format section

description: No output format specified; decision tree logic is described but result format is undefined
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section defining what a next-task recommendation looks like (task ID, title, reasoning, suggested first step)

description: Command takes $ARGUMENTS as required update content but has no guard for empty invocation; Claude behavior is undefined when no arguments given
suggested_fix: Add: If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user what they would like to update and which task

description: PR number via $ARGUMENTS is required; no guard for empty invocation
suggested_fix: Add: If $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask the user for the PR number before proceeding

description: Uses 'sufficient' (-2), 'clear' (-2), 'well-defined' (-2) as criteria for decision-making; creates ambiguity in when to parallelize vs serialize
suggested_fix: Replace 'sufficient context' with a concrete threshold (e.g., 'task has a description, acceptance criteria, and all dependencies are done'); replace 'well-defined' with 'has explicit acceptance criteria'

description: Uses 'relevant' and 'appropriate' as criteria for what information to include in task display
suggested_fix: Specify exactly which fields to always show vs. conditionally show based on task type

description: Uses 'relevant' and 'appropriate' in analysis mode descriptions
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit criteria for each analysis mode output

description: Uses 'comprehensive' and 'appropriate' in implementation strategy descriptions
suggested_fix: Replace with specific checklists for each implementation type

description: Uses 'intelligently' and 'appropriate' in command suggestion logic
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit if/then rules for each input pattern

description: Uses 'intelligently' to describe argument parsing
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit parsing rules: 'If argument matches status keyword X, filter by status. If argument is numeric range N-M, show tasks N through M.'

description: Uses 'intelligently' (-2) and 'appropriate' (-2) in task parsing description
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit parsing rules for each input pattern shown in the examples

description: Decision criteria uses 'acceptable' to describe code quality threshold for PARTIAL pass
suggested_fix: Replace 'Code quality is acceptable' with specific criteria: no unused variables, no any types without justification comment, error paths handled

description: Output format described only as 'briefly summarize what was implemented and suggest whether to continue'; no structured format specified
suggested_fix: Add explicit Output Format section with fields: task_id, status (done/blocked), summary (1-2 sentences), suggested_next_action

description: TASK ASSIGNMENT template shown as guidance but not prescribed as required output format for executor deployment messages
suggested_fix: Mark the TASK ASSIGNMENT block as Required Output Format and specify it must be used verbatim when deploying each executor

pattern: missing allowed-tools in all 47 plugin commands
description: No command in packages/claude-code-plugin/commands/ declares allowed-tools frontmatter; Claude may use any available tool
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools to each command; most commands need only Bash(task-master *) and mcp__task-master-ai__* scoped appropriately

description: Missing required `name` field in YAML frontmatter; NLPM scanner cannot register this command.
suggested_fix: Add `name: autosearch` (or chosen slug) to the frontmatter block.

description: npm installer wrapper executes `bash -c 'curl -fsSL <remote-install.sh> | bash'` without cryptographic verification; triggered when user runs `npx autosearch-ai` and autosearch is not installed.
suggested_fix: Verify install.sh checksum (SHA-256) before executing, or distribute autosearch solely via PyPI/pipx and remove the curl|bash path.

description: E2B sandbox setup string runs `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` to bootstrap uv without checksum verification; runs inside an isolated E2B sandbox but is an untrusted-download-and-exec pattern.
suggested_fix: Pin uv installer to a specific version and verify checksum, or pre-bake uv into the E2B sandbox Docker image.

description: `pip3 install git+https://github.com/0xmariowu/Autosearch.git` installs from the repository's HEAD with no version pin or commit hash; supply-chain risk if the repository is compromised between runs.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific tag or commit: `git+https://github.com/0xmariowu/Autosearch.git@vX.Y.Z` or use the PyPI release.

description: Installer appends `export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"` to user shell profiles (~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, ~/.profile) without a guard for headless/CI environments.
suggested_fix: Check `$CI` or `$TERM` env variables before writing to shell profiles; document the `--no-init` flag for CI users.

description: Pre-scan matched curl|sh pattern, but the text appears inside an `echo` message string — it is displayed as a hint to the user, not executed.
fp_reason: The curl|sh string is the argument to `echo`, not a shell command; it will never be executed by the script.
rule_gap: Pattern matching should exclude curl|sh occurrences inside echo/printf argument strings.

description: Pre-scan matched base64 decode+exec pattern. The code base64-encodes an internally-constructed Python script and sends it to an E2B sandbox via printf|base64 -d; the decoded content is a known-safe test script, not user-supplied data.
fp_reason: base64 is used as a transport mechanism to pass a local Python string to a remote E2B sandbox over stdin; the content is constructed in the same file with no external input.
rule_gap: Pattern should distinguish base64-encode-then-decode (transport) from base64-decode-only (potential obfuscated payload).

description: Missing `name` field in YAML frontmatter; required for NL artifact registration.
suggested_fix: Add `name: autosearch` to the frontmatter.

description: No explicit empty-input guard; `$ARGUMENTS` is passed directly to `run_clarify` with no check for empty string.
suggested_fix: Add: `If the user provides no query, ask: "What topic or question should AutoSearch research?"` before calling run_clarify.

description: Agent has no complete input/output examples; only an output template is shown without a real worked example.
suggested_fix: Add one complete worked example with a realistic diff excerpt as input and the expected table as output.

description: Vague qualifier "adequately" in the description — does not specify the threshold or criteria for adequacy.
suggested_fix: Replace "adequately" with a concrete criterion, e.g. "at least one assertion per new error path or boundary condition".

description: Missing "When to Choose It", "How To Search", and "Known Quirks" sections present in the canonical channel skill format.
suggested_fix: Add standard structural sections following the hackernews or weibo channel template.

description: "How To Search" is a single sentence; body mixes Chinese and English without a language-tagging convention. No method documentation for the HTML parsing approach.
suggested_fix: Write a full "How To Search" section in English following the project standard; move Chinese prose to a Known Quirks note.

description: Body contains only three short sentences and two stub Known Quirks bullets. Missing "When to Choose It" and "How To Search" sections.
suggested_fix: Expand body with When/How/Quirks sections following the standard channel template.

description: `experience_digest` field uses `experience/experience.md` (subdirectory) while project-wide convention is `experience.md` (sibling file).
suggested_fix: Change `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: `experience_digest` field uses `experience/experience.md` (subdirectory) while project-wide convention is `experience.md` (sibling file).
suggested_fix: Change `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: "How To Search (Planned)" repeats the `algolia_search` method name as three separate bullets with no distinguishing content.
suggested_fix: Merge the three bullets into one structured description of what algolia_search captures.

description: Body references a `via_douyin_mcp` method and `cookie:douyin` dependency not present in frontmatter `methods` or `fallback_chain`.
suggested_fix: Either add `via_douyin_mcp` as a declared method in frontmatter or remove the implementation description from the body.

description: `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` deviates from all other skills in the codebase which use `experience_digest: experience.md`; may prevent runtime experience loader from finding the digest file.
suggested_fix: Align to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: `experience_digest: experience/experience.md` deviates from all other skills in the codebase which use `experience_digest: experience.md`; may prevent runtime experience loader from finding the digest file.
suggested_fix: Align to `experience_digest: experience.md`.

description: Body describes a `via_douyin_mcp` implementation path with `cookie:douyin` dependency but neither appears in the frontmatter `methods` list or `fallback_chain`; the described behavior is unreachable via the standard routing mechanism.
suggested_fix: Add `via_douyin_mcp` as a declared method or clearly label the body section as future work.

description: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add 2 <example> blocks: one for P9 multi-agent orchestration scenario, one for task decomposition with P8 team spawn.

description: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add 2 <example> blocks: one for receiving a sub-task from P8, one for the [P7-COMPLETION] delivery flow.

description: Agent has no <example> blocks. R09 requires minimum 2 examples per agent for reliable triggering.
suggested_fix: Add 2 <example> blocks: one for large project (5+ agent) kickoff, one for P9 conflict arbitration.

description: Agent does not declare model field. P9 orchestration and prompt-writing requires reasoning capability (sonnet minimum).
suggested_fix: Add 'model: sonnet' to frontmatter.

description: Agent does not declare model field. P7 execution tasks are reasoning-intensive; model choice affects cost/quality tradeoff.
suggested_fix: Add 'model: sonnet' to frontmatter (or 'model: haiku' if cost-optimizing execution tasks).

description: Agent body defines narrative tags ([P9-分配], [P9-验收]) but no structured output template for Sprint summaries or status reports. R12 requires output format defined in body.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format section with Sprint report template showing iteration summary, P8 status, blockers, next assignment.

description: Agent body defines narrative tags ([P10-编制], [P10-断事]) but no structured output template. R12 requires output format defined in body.
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format section with strategic input template and team topology diagram format.

description: Stub skill (4 lines) has no examples. R06: no examples at all in a technical skill = -10. Current pattern delegates entirely to references/p7-protocol.md without orientation.
suggested_fix: Add at least one worked example showing a P7 task receipt and [P7-COMPLETION] delivery, even if brief. Or inline the protocol key sections.

description: Stub skill has no examples. Delegates to references/p9-protocol.md without any orientation content.
suggested_fix: Add one example showing P9 task decomposition into Task Prompt with six elements.

description: Stub skill has no examples. Delegates to references/p10-protocol.md without any orientation content.
suggested_fix: Add one example showing P10 strategic input template for a multi-P9 project.

description: Stub skill body has no output format specification. The format is defined externally in p7-protocol.md, which is not loaded by the skill itself.
suggested_fix: Add one-paragraph note: 'Output format: see [P7-COMPLETION] block template in references/p7-protocol.md §交付协议.'

description: Stub skill body has no output format specification.
suggested_fix: Add one-paragraph note referencing the Task Prompt 六要素 template in p9-protocol.md.

description: Stub skill body has no output format specification.
suggested_fix: Add one-paragraph note referencing the 战略输入模板 in p10-protocol.md.

description: Multi-step workflow (read file → ask questions per section → collect responses → write JSON → upload) written as a single run-on sentence with no numbered steps. R14: multi-step body with no numbered steps = -10.
suggested_fix: Break into 4 numbered steps: 1. Read references/survey.md, 2. Use AskUserQuestion per section, 3. Collect and aggregate responses as JSON, 4. Write to ~/.pua/survey-response.json and upload.

description: No output format defined. After survey completion the user sees no defined confirmation structure.
suggested_fix: Add output template: '> [PUA SURVEY] 感谢完成问卷。已写入 ~/.pua/survey-response.json 并上传。'

description: Skill body is 449 lines, in the 400–500 penalty range. R05: over 400 lines starts to create context bloat.
suggested_fix: Split the 13 flavor methodology sections into a separate loadable reference file, bringing the core skill under 350 lines.

description: Agent instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p9-protocol.md'. The agent file is at agents/; the actual protocol file is at skills/pua/references/p9-protocol.md. The relative reference will silently fail.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Use Glob to find **/pua/skills/pua/references/p9-protocol.md then Read it.'

description: Agent instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p10-protocol.md'. File does not exist at agents/references/; it is at skills/pua/references/p10-protocol.md.
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Use Glob to find **/pua/skills/pua/references/p10-protocol.md then Read it.'

description: Agent uses correct glob for skills/pua/SKILL.md but then instructs 'cat 同目录下的 references/p7-protocol.md' which resolves to agents/references/ — wrong directory.
suggested_fix: Change to: 'Use Glob **/pua/skills/pua/references/p7-protocol.md then Read it.' — consistent with the glob pattern already used for SKILL.md.

description: Script executes: bash "$(cat /tmp/pua-plugin-root)/hooks/sanitize-session.sh". The /tmp/pua-plugin-root file is written in the same hook and could be substituted by a local attacker between write and exec (TOCTOU). If /tmp/ is world-writable, the path can be redirected to an arbitrary script.
suggested_fix: Replace /tmp lookup with direct ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-$(ls -td ~/.claude/plugins/cache/pua-skills/pua/*/ | head -1)} resolution without a temp file.

description: Sanitized session JSONL uploaded to third-party domain pua-skill.pages.dev via Python urllib. Upload is consent-gated (user selects '很有用 — 上传评分 + 脱敏 session') but the upload destination is not user-controlled and the sanitization happens client-side before transfer.
suggested_fix: Consider making the upload endpoint configurable (${PUA_FEEDBACK_ENDPOINT:-https://pua-skill.pages.dev/api/feedback}) or documenting the data retention policy prominently in the consent UI.

description: Leaderboard registration sends email and phone number to pua-skill.pages.dev/api/leaderboard. The script is generated inline as LLM output (not a pre-written script), so the user cannot inspect it before it runs. PII leaves the machine to a third-party endpoint.
suggested_fix: Add explicit in-UI disclosure of data retention policy before collecting PII. Make phone number optional at the data-collection step, not just marked as '选填' in the question text.

description: python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$PUA_CONFIG'))..." interpolates $PUA_CONFIG into a Python source string. A config path containing single quotes breaks the expression; other special chars may cause unexpected behavior.
suggested_fix: Use python3 -c "import os,json; print(json.load(open(os.path.expanduser('~/.pua/config.json')))...)" with a hardcoded expanduser path instead.

description: Same python3 -c with $PUA_CONFIG path interpolation as failure-detector.sh:10. Affects all hooks that source this pattern.
suggested_fix: Use hardcoded os.path.expanduser('~/.pua/config.json') instead of $PUA_CONFIG interpolation.

description: Uses python3 -c with path interpolation in some branches. The main always_on check at line 28 uses $CONFIG variable interpolated into python source.
suggested_fix: Use os.path.expanduser with hardcoded path in all python3 -c calls in this file.

description: timeout 120 bash -c "$VERIFY_CMD" executes a user-provided verification command from the loop state file. The command is set by the user at /pua-loop setup time, so this is intentional design. Noted for completeness: any local file-write to the state file could inject arbitrary commands.
suggested_fix: No change required for the primary threat model (user's own machine). If multi-user or shared-workspace environments are a concern, add a hash-based integrity check on the state file before reading VERIFY_CMD.

description: Root CLAUDE.md uses unprefixed tool names (execute, search, batch_execute, execute_file, fetch_and_index) throughout Tool Selection and Rules sections; actual MCP tool names carry ctx_ prefix (ctx_execute, ctx_search, etc.) confirmed by hooks.json matchers
suggested_fix: Replace all unprefixed tool names with ctx_ equivalents to match actual MCP registration names

description: 'UNSURE' used as a branch condition in decision tree at line 50; ambiguous threshold that could cause inconsistent tool routing
suggested_fix: Replace 'UNSURE' with explicit condition such as 'output may exceed 20 lines or command is not on whitelist'

description: execSync called with shell:true and interpolated ${pkg} variable; pkg is currently hardcoded as 'better-sqlite3' but the shell:true creates command injection surface that would be exploitable if pkg ever receives external input
suggested_fix: Replace execSync template literal with execFileSync(['npm', 'install', pkg, '--no-package-lock', '--no-save', '--silent'], {cwd, stdio, timeout}) to eliminate shell injection surface

description: postinstall lifecycle hook auto-executes scripts/postinstall.mjs on every npm install without user confirmation; script invokes system commands (where, mklink /J via cmd.exe) on Windows for nvm4w junction creation
suggested_fix: Document postinstall behavior prominently in README; consider making Windows junction creation opt-in via explicit CLI command rather than automatic postinstall

description: npm install executed at hook runtime when better-sqlite3 binary is absent; installs packages over network inside the agent's event loop without explicit user consent or progress visibility
suggested_fix: Emit a console.error warning to stderr before install so user is aware; add CONTEXT_MODE_NO_AUTO_INSTALL env var to disable the auto-install path

description: Second execSync with shell:true for npm rebuild better-sqlite3; args are fully hardcoded strings reducing actual risk but pattern is still dangerous
suggested_fix: Replace with execFileSync(['npm', 'rebuild', 'better-sqlite3', '--ignore-scripts=false'], {cwd, stdio, timeout})

description: Self-heal block writes to ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json (line 88) and ~/.claude/settings.json (line 146) in user home directory; silently modifies Claude Code configuration without user knowledge
suggested_fix: Add opt-out env var (e.g. CONTEXT_MODE_NO_SELF_HEAL); emit console.error diagnostic when configuration is modified so user is aware

description: NODE_PATH unconditionally exported with plugin node_modules prepended; affects all child Node.js processes spawned during diagnostic run and could shadow system modules
suggested_fix: Pass NODE_PATH as prefix on specific node invocations rather than exporting globally: NODE_PATH=... node ...

description: execSync(`codesign --sign - --force "${binaryPath}"`) runs through system shell with binaryPath derived from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var; double-quotes provide basic protection but env var origin is untrusted
suggested_fix: Use execFileSync(['codesign', '--sign', '-', '--force', binaryPath]) to avoid shell interpretation entirely

description: Core dependencies use ^ semver ranges: better-sqlite3 ^12.6.2 (native module), @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.26.0, zod ^3.25.0; native module especially risky as minor updates can introduce ABI breaks or supply-chain compromise
suggested_fix: Pin better-sqlite3 to exact version; run pnpm audit in CI; consider exact pinning for all production dependencies

description: Root CLAUDE.md documents bare tool names (execute, search, batch_execute) while all other artifacts use ctx_-prefixed names confirmed canonical by hooks.json MCP matchers and configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md; root CLAUDE.md appears to be a stale pre-ctx_-prefix copy
suggested_fix: Update root CLAUDE.md to use ctx_ prefix throughout Tool Selection and Rules sections; verify it matches configs/claude-code/CLAUDE.md tool inventory

description: CLAUDE.md is effectively empty (1 line with no content); provides no project context, conventions, build instructions, or agent architecture overview to Claude sessions

description: 13 thin crypto agents all delegate via 'Read and Execute' to prompt files in .claude/commands/agent_prompts/; the prompt files lack frontmatter and are undiscoverable as standalone commands; if prompt files are moved the agents break silently
suggested_fix: Either consolidate instructions into agent files or add frontmatter to all prompt files and document the split pattern in CLAUDE.md

description: sentient.md description field says 'Manage, organize and ships your codebase' but body content is explicitly a destructive rm -rf demo; creates routing confusion for auto-delegation
suggested_fix: Update description to accurately reflect the rm -rf demo purpose

description: cook.md calls agents by base name (crypto-coin-analyzer, crypto-market-agent) without specifying tier; three tier variants exist (haiku/sonnet/opus) with identical descriptions; selection is non-deterministic
suggested_fix: Specify agent tier explicitly: crypto-coin-analyzer-sonnet (or whichever tier is intended)

description: Root CLAUDE.md is empty; the project has complex hook infrastructure, 19 agents, and 22 commands but no session-level context is provided to orient Claude; every session starts blind
suggested_fix: Add project overview covering: uv tool chain, hook architecture, agent tier system, and key commands

description: postinstall script runs `node ./bin/install.js` automatically at npm install time; bin/ directory does not exist in repo but pattern enables arbitrary code execution in any published version
suggested_fix: Remove or gate the postinstall with a confirmation prompt; create bin/install.js with safe, auditable installation logic before publishing to npm

description: SessionStart hook executes ./scripts/session-init.sh on every Claude Code session start; this script does not exist in the repository, creating a phantom execution point
suggested_fix: Create scripts/session-init.sh with documented, minimal safe content, or remove the SessionStart hook until the script is implemented and reviewed

pattern: missing hook script via env var
description: SessionStart hook executes ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/session-init.sh; script does not exist; env-variable path resolution adds an injection surface if CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is attacker-controlled
suggested_fix: Create the script or remove the hook; validate CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT before use if retained

description: MCP server context7 invoked via `npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@latest`; unpinned version downloaded and auto-executed at every MCP startup without integrity verification
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific release, e.g. @upstash/context7-mcp@1.0.0, and verify the package signature

description: MCP server sequential-thinking invoked via `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking` with no version pin; auto-downloads at startup
suggested_fix: Pin to a verified release version to prevent silent upgrades introducing malicious code

description: subprocess.run() calls git commands using list form (no shell=True); safe in current form but --framework-repo URL is a caller-controlled argument passed to git clone
suggested_fix: Validate framework-repo URL against an allowlist (e.g. https://github.com/*) before passing to subprocess

description: update script runs `node ./bin/update.js`; same missing-file issue as postinstall but lower severity since it is not auto-executed at install time
suggested_fix: Create bin/update.js with auditable content before publishing, or remove the script if not needed

description: File starts with bare `name: sc:agent` on line 1 without an opening --- delimiter; Claude Code cannot parse this as valid YAML frontmatter so the command fails to register

suggested_fix: Create the script or remove the hook; validate CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT before use if retained

description: MCP server context7 invoked via `npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@latest`; unpinned version downloaded and auto-executed at every MCP startup without integrity verification
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific release, e.g. @upstash/context7-mcp@1.0.0, and verify the package signature

description: MCP server sequential-thinking invoked via `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking` with no version pin; auto-downloads at startup
suggested_fix: Pin to a verified release version to prevent silent upgrades introducing malicious code

description: subprocess.run() calls git commands using list form (no shell=True); safe in current form but --framework-repo URL is a caller-controlled argument passed to git clone
suggested_fix: Validate framework-repo URL against an allowlist (e.g. https://github.com/*) before passing to subprocess

description: update script runs `node ./bin/update.js`; same missing-file issue as postinstall but lower severity since it is not auto-executed at install time
suggested_fix: Create bin/update.js with auditable content before publishing, or remove the script if not needed

description: File starts with bare `name: sc:agent` on line 1 without an opening --- delimiter; Claude Code cannot parse this as valid YAML frontmatter so the command fails to register
suggested_fix: Add --- as the first line of the file to open the frontmatter block

description: Same broken frontmatter as src counterpart; missing opening --- causes registration failure
suggested_fix: Add --- as the first line of the file

description: SessionStart hook command `./scripts/session-init.sh` references a file that does not exist anywhere in the repository; hook will error on every session start

description: SessionStart hook references session-init.sh via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/session-init.sh; script does not exist; hook errors on session start

description: YAML config with command metadata is inside a fenced code block in the document body rather than actual YAML frontmatter; name and description are not parsed by Claude Code

description: README.md lists only 3 agents (deep-research, repo-index, self-review) but the directory contains 21 files including 18 additional agent definitions; severely misleading for contributors
suggested_fix: Regenerate the Available Agents list from the actual directory contents

description: Help command displays SuperClaude v4.1.7 / Python 0.4.0 but package.json and CLAUDE.md state v4.3.0; users see outdated version in /sc help
suggested_fix: Update version strings to 4.3.0 / 0.0.0 (or whatever the current pypi version is)

description: sync_from_framework.py is designed to add sc- prefix to agent names when syncing to plugins/ directory, but all plugins/superclaude/agents/ files have the same unprefixed names as src/; the transform is not being run, breaking intended namespace isolation
suggested_fix: Run the sync script and verify plugins/ agent frontmatter names have sc- prefix; or document that the prefix transform was intentionally dropped

description: Both src/ and plugins/ hooks.json reference scripts/session-init.sh which does not exist in any location in the repository; the hook is completely broken for all users
suggested_fix: Create a minimal session-init.sh or remove the SessionStart hook

description: pm.md (command) and pm-agent.md (agent) both define Serena MCP session-start protocols using the same memory keys (pm_context, last_session, next_actions) with no canonical owner specified; concurrent activation could cause write conflicts
suggested_fix: Designate one artifact as the session protocol owner; have the other defer to it via explicit reference

description: API examples use `type: Opaque` which contradicts the explicit warning at lines 56–70 that Opaque breaks Jenkins credential sync; correct type is credential.devops.kubesphere.io/basic-auth or the matching domain-specific type.
suggested_fix: Replace `"type": "Opaque"` with the correct credential.devops.kubesphere.io type on lines 111, 134, and 469.

description: YAML template syntax error: `low: "{{low}}` is missing closing double-quote; produces invalid YAML that will fail to parse when applied as an InstallPlan.
suggested_fix: Change to: low: "{{low}}"

description: Placeholder comment says '# From Step 3' but the installation section defines only Step 1 and Step 2; agents will look for a non-existent step.
suggested_fix: Change '# From Step 3' to '# From Step 2'.

description: Bold markdown marker is not closed: `**Step 3b: For Private Repository (with credential):` is missing its closing `**`; renders incorrectly and may confuse section boundary parsing.
suggested_fix: Add closing ** at end of the line: `**Step 3b: For Private Repository (with credential):**`

description: 'Project Components' ASCII architecture diagram block is duplicated; the same content block appears twice in the file starting at line 92.
suggested_fix: Remove the second occurrence of the duplicated Project Components section.

description: Uses `xargs -n 3 sh -c 'kubectl patch $2 -n $0 $1...'` with resource names piped from kubectl output; resource names are interpolated directly into a shell string, matching the HIGH shell injection pattern.
suggested_fix: Replace xargs sh -c with a while-read loop: `while read -r ns name crd; do kubectl patch "$crd" -n "$ns" "$name" ...; done` to avoid shell string interpolation.

description: Prints OpenSearch password to stdout: `echo "  Password: ${OS_PASSWORD}"` where OS_PASSWORD is read from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret; credential leaks to terminal scrollback and shell logs.
suggested_fix: Replace with `echo "  Password: [redacted — read from vector-sinks secret]"` or suppress the password output entirely.

description: OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token (TOKEN_FILE) without setting restrictive file permissions; token is readable by other users on shared systems.
suggested_fix: Add os.chmod(TOKEN_FILE, 0o600) immediately after writing the token file.

description: Identical to kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py (file duplicated verbatim); OAuth token cached to ~/.kubesphere_token without restrictive file permissions; same insecure token storage pattern.
suggested_fix: Add os.chmod(TOKEN_FILE, 0o600) after writing the token; consolidate duplicate ks_api.py into a shared module to prevent fix-one-miss-one maintenance problems.

description: Password 'P@88w0rd' hardcoded in documentation API examples at lines 130, 803, and 867; realistic-looking credential may be copied into production configurations.
suggested_fix: Replace all occurrences of 'P@88w0rd' with '<YOUR_SECURE_PASSWORD>' placeholder.

description: Common Issues troubleshooting table duplicated at lines 640–649 and 677–684; identical rows appear twice, bloating the file and creating a maintenance hazard.
suggested_fix: Remove the second occurrence of the duplicate Common Issues table.

description: Common Mistakes partial table at lines 1277–1282 duplicates content that appears earlier in the file.
suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate partial table section at lines 1277–1282.

description: 'Key Resources' heading appears twice in the file, creating an ambiguous document structure and confusing section navigation.
suggested_fix: Remove the second Key Resources section or merge its content under a single heading.

description: The Authorization header `-H "Authorization: Bearer ${API_TOKEN}"` appears twice in the List Pipelines curl command (lines 247–249); the HTTP request contains a duplicate header.
suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate Authorization header line from the curl example.

description: Hardcoded example password 'P@88w0rd' at lines 130, 803, and 867 appears realistic; readers may use it in production without substitution.
suggested_fix: Use a clearly synthetic placeholder such as '<YOUR_SECURE_PASSWORD>' throughout all examples.

description: Uses vague word 'appropriate' at line 713 without defining what an appropriate value means in this context; readers cannot determine compliance.
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate' with a specific, measurable criterion (e.g., 'at least 2 replicas for HA' or a concrete range).

description: skills/kubesphere-core/scripts/ks_api.py and skills/kubesphere-multi-tenant-management/scripts/ks_api.py are byte-for-byte identical; security fixes must be applied to both files, creating a maintenance hazard.
suggested_fix: Extract to a shared utility location referenced by both skills, or document the intentional duplication with a synchronisation policy.

description: generate-config.sh reads OpenSearch credentials from the vector-sinks Kubernetes Secret (created by the vector extension) but this cross-extension runtime dependency is not documented in whizard-telemetry/SKILL.md or vector/SKILL.md; agents invoking the script without vector installed will get silent failures.
suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite note in whizard-telemetry/SKILL.md stating that generate-config.sh requires the vector extension's vector-sinks Secret to be present.
